mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers discount rates, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines but 5-day forward returns in 80%+ of cases are negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs baseline).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals strong labor market conditions that increase corporate earnings visibility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 4.0% but 20-day forward returns in 70%+ of cases are negative (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals robust supply-side expansion that drives corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO and GDPC1 both rise but 60-day forward returns are negative in 60%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% but 20-day forward returns are positive in 70%+ of cases (Rank IC > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1,300 thousand units signals strong demand for construction, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST > 1300.0 but 5-day forward returns are negative in 60%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS rises but 20-day forward returns in 70%+ of cases are negative (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and declining unemployment signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS up and UNRATE down but 60-day forward returns are negative in 50%+ of cases (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 2.35 trillion dollars indicates strong economic momentum that drives equity earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 23500 but 60-day forward returns in 60%+ of cases are negative (Rank IC < 0.03).
